title: 2010年全国硕士研究生招生考试英语（一）真题

questions.article:
In 1924 America’s National Research Council sent two engineers to supervise a series of experiments at a telephone-parts factory called the Hawthorne Plant near Chicago. It hoped they would learn how shop-floor lighting _1_ workers’ productivity. Instead, the studies ended _2_ giving their name to the “Hawthorne effect”, the extremely influential idea that the very _3_ of being experimented upon changed subjects’ behavior.
The idea arose because of the _4_ behavior of the women in the plant. According to _5_ of the experiments, their hourly output rose when lighting was increased, but also when it was dimmed. It did not _6_ what was done in the experiment; _7_ something was changed, productivity rose. A (an) _8_ that they were being experimented upon seemed to be _9_ to alter workers’ behavior _10_ itself.
After several decades, the same data were _11_ to econometric analysis. The Hawthorne experiments had another surprise in store. _12_ the descriptions on record, no systematic _13_ was found that levels of productivity were related to changes in lighting.
It turns out that the peculiar way of conducting the experiments may have led to _14_ interpretations of what happened. _15_, lighting was always changed on a Sunday. When work started again on Monday, output _16_ rose compared with the previous Saturday and _17_ to rise for the next couple of days. _18_, a comparison with data for weeks when there was no experimentation showed that output always went up on Mondays. Workers _19_ to be diligent for the first few days of the week in any case, before _20_ a plateau and then slackening off. This suggests that the alleged “Hawthorne effect” is hard to pin down.

sub_questions.analysis: 【整体分析】来源：The Economist《经济学人》于2009年6月4日刊登的文章Light Work（照明工作）。主题：本文主要探讨了1924年霍桑实验和随后对其的分析。实验发现，照明的变化似乎影响工人的生产效率，但后来的研究未能找到系统性的证据支持这一观点。文章指出实验设计中的特殊方式可能导致对结果的误导性理解，而每周一的产量上升可能是由于工人在一周开始时努力工作的常见模式。因此，文章质疑了所谓的“霍桑效应”的可验证性。结构：【试题解析】【选项释义】A. affected 影响	B. achieved 达到C. extracted 提取；提炼	D. restored 恢复【考查点】动词辨析和上下文句意。【解题思路】空格处需要填入动词，与workers’ productivity（工人生产效率）构成动宾逻辑，由下文their hourly output rose when lighting was increased, but also when it was dimmed（当照明增强时，她们的每小时产量上升，但是当照明变暗时，产量也同样上升）可知，该实验是为了研究车间照明是如何影响工人生产效率的。因此A选项正确。【干扰项排除】B、C、D选项均不符合语境。【句意】希望他们能弄清楚车间照明对工人生产效率的影响。

【选项释义】A. at （end ~）在某时刻结束 B. up （end ~）最终成为C. with （end ~）以……结束 D. off （end ~）结束；终止【考查点】固定搭配和句内语义。【解题思路】空格处需要填入介词，与ended进行搭配，由giving their name to the “Hawthorne effect”（ 提出了一个极具影响力的概念——“霍桑效应”）可知，此处表示这些研究最终的结果是提出了“霍桑效应”这一概念。end up是固定搭配，意为“最终成为”，因此B选项正确。【干扰项排除】A、C、D选项均不符合语境。【句意】然而，这些研究最终提出了一个极具影响力的概念——“霍桑效应”。

【选项释义】A. truth 真相；实情 B. sight 视力；视觉C. act 行为；行动 D. proof 证据；证明【考查点】名字辨析和句内语义。【解题思路】空格处需要填入名词，与changed（改变）构成主谓逻辑，由of being experimented upon（被用来做实验的）可知，此处表示受试行为可以改变实验对象的行为。因此C选项正确。【干扰项排除】A、B、D选项均不符合语境。【句意】这个概念认为，受试行为本身可以改变实验对象的行为。

【选项释义】A. controversial 引起争论的；有争议的	B. perplexing 令人不解的C. mischievous 顽皮的；捣蛋的	D. ambiguous 模棱两可的；含混不清的【考查点】名词辨析和上下文语义。【解题思路】空格处需要填入形容词作定语，修饰behavior（行为），由下文their hourly output rose when lighting was increased, but also when it was dimmed（当照明增强时，她们的每小时产量上升，但是当照明变暗时，产量也同样上升）可知，无论照明的变化，工厂女工的产量都在上升，这种行为是令人困惑的。因此B选项正确。【干扰项排除】A、C、D选项均不符合语境。【句意】这个观念的提出源于工厂女工们令人困惑的行为。

【选项释义】A. requirements 必要条件 B. explanations 解释；说明C. accounts 描述；叙述 D. assessments 看法；评估【考查点】名词辨析和句内语义。【解题思路】空格处需要填入名词，与of the experiments进行搭配，由their hourly output rose when lighting was increased, but also when it was dimmed（当照明增强时，她们的每小时产量上升，但是当照明变暗时，产量也同样上升）可知，这是对实验观察的描述。因此C选项正确。【干扰项排除】A、B、D选项均不符合语境。【句意】根据实验的描述，当照明增强时，她们的每小时产量上升，但是当照明变暗时，产量也同样上升。

【选项释义】A. conclude 推断出；得出结论 B. matter 要紧；有重大影响C. indicate 表明；显示 D. work 工作；劳动【考查点】动词辨析和句内语义。【解题思路】空格处需要填入动词，与主语从句what was done in the experiment（实验中做了什么）构成主谓逻辑，由something was changed, productivity rose（某些东西有所改变，生产效率就会提高）可知，此处表示实验中的做法并不重要，因此B选项正确。【干扰项排除】A、C、D选项均不符合语境。【句意】实验中做了什么并不重要。

【选项释义】A. as far as 就……而言 B. for fear that 唯恐；以免C. in case that 万一；如果 D. so long as 只要【考查点】词组辨析和句内逻辑。【解题思路】空格处需要填入词组，表示此处存在的逻辑关系，由It did not matter what was done in the experiment（实验中做了什么并不重要）可知，只要某些东西有所改变是生产效率提高的条件，因此D选项正确。【干扰项排除】A、B、C选项均不能表示条件关系。【句意】只要某些东西有所改变，生产效率就会提高。

【选项释义】A. awareness 认识；意识 B. expectation 预料；预期C. sentiment 看法；情绪 D. illusion 幻想【考查点】名词辨析和句内语义。【解题思路】空格处需要填入名词作主语，that引导同位语从句解释说明空格处，由they were being experimented upon（他们正被作为实验对象）可知，此处表示工人们意识到了自己正被作为实验对象，因此A选项正确。【干扰项排除】B、C、D选项均不符合语境。【句意】似乎意识到自己正被作为实验对象这一点。

【选项释义】A. suitable 合适的；适宜的 B. excessive 过分的；过度的C. enough 足够的；充分的 D. abundant 大量的；丰盛的【考查点】形容词辨析和上下文语义。【解题思路】空格处需要填入形容词，作主语An awareness that they were being experimented upon（意识到自己正被作为实验对象）的表语，由上文so long as something was changed, productivity rose（只要某些东西有所改变，生产效率就会提高）可知，此处表示意识到自己正被作为实验对象已经足够改变工人的行为，因此C选项正确。【干扰项排除】A、B、D选项均不符合语境。【句意】似乎意识到自己正被作为实验对象这一点已足以改变工人们的行为。

【选项释义】A. about 关于 B. for 为了C. on 在……上 D. by 凭借【考查点】介词辨析和和句内语义。【解题思路】空格处需要填入介词，与itself进行搭配，itself强调意识到自己正被作为实验对象这一点本身，此处表示仅仅凭借这一点就能改变工人的行为，因此D选项正确。【干扰项排除】A、B、C选项均不符合语境。【句意】似乎意识到自己正被作为实验对象这一点已足以改变工人们的行为。

【选项释义】A. compared 比较；对比	B. shown 表明；证明C. subjected 使经受	D. conveyed 表达；传递【考查点】动词辨析和句内语义。【解题思路】空格处需要填入动词的过去分词形式，构成被动语态，由econometric analysis（计量经济学分析）可知，此处表示数据被用于计量经济学分析，因此C选项正确。【干扰项排除】A、B、D选项均不符合语境。【句意】几十年后，同样的数据被用于计量经济学分析。

【选项释义】A. Contrary to 与……相反 B. Consistent with 与……一致C. Parallel with 与……平行 D. Peculiar to 特有的；独特于【考查点】词组辨析和上下文语义。【解题思路】空格处需要填入词组，由上文The Hawthorne experiments had another surprise in store.（霍桑实验又带来了另一个令人吃惊的结果。）可知，令人吃惊表明出现的结果与原来的实验结论是相反的，因此A选项正确。【干扰项排除】B、C、D选项均不符合语境。【句意】与记录中的描述相反。

【选项释义】A. evidence 证明；证据 B. guidance 指导；引导C. implication 含意；暗指 D. source 来源；出处【考查点】名词辨析和句内语义。【解题思路】空格处需要填入名词作主语，由that levels of productivity were related to changes in lighting（生产效率的高低与照明变化有关）可知，此处表示没有找到表明生产效率的高低与照明变化有关的系统性证据，因此A选项正确。【干扰项排除】B、C、D选项均不符合语境。【句意】与记录中的描述相反，没有找到系统性的证据表明生产效率的高低与照明变化有关。

【选项释义】A. disputable 可质疑的；有争辩余地的	B. enlightening 启迪的C. reliable 可信赖的；可依靠的	D. misleading 误导的；引入歧途的【考查点】形容词辨析和上下文语义。【解题思路】空格处需要填入形容词作定语，修饰interpretations（理解），上文说到so long as something was changed, productivity rose（只要某些东西有所改变，生产效率就会提高），接着说到no systematic evidence was found that levels of productivity were related to changes in lighting（没有找到系统性的证据表明生产效率的高低与照明变化有关），两个结论矛盾，说明这种做实验的特殊方式可能会对事实产生误解，因此D选项正确。【干扰项排除】A、B、C选项均不符合语境。【句意】结果表明，实验的特殊方式可能导致对所发生情况的误导性理解。

【选项释义】A. In contrast 相比之下 B. For example 例如C. In consequence 因此；结果 D. As usual 像往常一样【考查点】词组辨析和句内逻辑。【解题思路】空格处需要填入词组，上文说到It turns out that the peculiar way of conducting the experiments may have led to misleading interpretations of what happened.（结果表明，实验的特殊方式可能导致对所发生情况的误导性理解。），下文的两个例子，即调节照明和没有调节照明时产量都提高了，证明了照明变化与生产率无关，是对上文观点的举例说明，因此B选项正确。【干扰项排除】A、C、D选项均不符合语境。【句意】例如，照明总是在星期日进行变动。

【选项释义】A. duly 按时地；准时地 B. accidentally 意外地；偶然地C. unpredictably 无法预言地；不可预见地 D. suddenly 突然；忽然【考查点】副词辨析和句上下文语义。【解题思路】空格处需要填入副词修饰rose（上升），由下文output always went up on Mondays（每个星期一产量总是会上升）可知，周一产量会上升是确定的，因此A选项正确。【干扰项排除】B、C、D选项均不符合语境。【句意】当星期一重新开始工作时，产量相比上个星期六会如期上升。

【选项释义】A. failed 失败；未能 B. ceased 终止C. started 开始 D. continued 继续【考查点】动词辨析和句内语义。【解题思路】空格处需要填入动词，与to rise进行搭配，由output duly rose compared with the previous Saturday（产量相比上个星期六会如期上升）可知，此处表示接下来几天的产量会继续上升，因此D选项正确。【干扰项排除】A、B、C选项均不符合语境。【句意】当星期一重新开始工作时，产量相比上个星期六会如期上升，并且在接下来的几天里会继续上升。

【选项释义】A. Therefore 因此；所以 B. Furthermore 此外；而且C. However 然而 D. Meanwhile 与此同时【考查点】副词辨析和上下文逻辑。【解题思路】空格处需要填入副词，表示此处存在的逻辑关系，上文说到When work started again on Monday, output duly rose（当星期一重新开始工作时，产量会如期上升），而下文又说a comparison with data for weeks when there was no experimentation showed that output always went up on Mondays（与没有进行实验那几周的数据进行比较表明，每个星期一产量总是会上升），一般来说做实验和不做实验的结果应该是不一样的，所以此处表示转折的逻辑关系，因此C选项正确。【干扰项排除】A、B、D选项均不能表示转折的逻辑关系。【句意】然而，与没有进行实验那几周的数据进行比较表明，每个星期一产量总是会上升。

【选项释义】A. attempted 试图；尝试 B. tended 往往会；常常就C. chose 选择；挑选 D. intended 打算；计划【考查点】动词辨析和上下语义。【解题思路】空格处需要填入动词，与to be diligent进行搭配，由上文output always went up on Mondays（每个星期一产量总是会上升）可知，此处表示不管在什么情况下，在一周的开始几天，工人们往往会努力工作，因此B选项正确。【干扰项排除】A、C、D选项均不符合语境。【句意】不管在什么情况下，在一周的开始几天，工人们往往会努力工作。

【选项释义】A. breaking 弄坏；损坏 B. climbing 攀登；爬C. surpassing 超过；胜过 D. hitting 达到【考查点】动词辨析和句内语义。【解题思路】空格处需要填入动词，与a plateau（一个平台期）构成动宾逻辑，由Workers tended to be diligent for the first few days of the week in any case（不管在什么情况下，在一周的开始几天，工人们往往会努力工作）可知，接下来的发展应该是达到稳定的平台期，因此D选项正确。【干扰项排除】A、B、C选项均不符合语境。【句意】不管在什么情况下，在一周的开始几天，工人们往往会努力工作，然后达到一个平台期，接着逐渐松懈。【重点词汇】perplexing 	/pərˈplɛksɪŋ/ 	adj. 令人困惑的influential 	/ˌɪnfluˈɛnʃəl/ 	adj. 有影响力的alter 	/ˈɔːltər/ 	v. 改变awareness 	/əˈwɛərnəs/ 	n. 意识econometric 	/ˌiːkənəˈmɛtrɪk/ 	adj. 计量经济学的systematic 	/ˌsɪstəˈmætɪk/ 	adj. 系统的productivity 	/ˌproʊˌdʌkˈtɪvəti/ 	n. 生产力peculiar 	/pɪˈkjuːliər/ 	adj. 奇怪的misleading 	/mɪsˈlidɪŋ/ 	adj. 误导性的interpretation 	/ɪnˌtɜːrprɪˈteɪʃən/ 	n. 解释diligent 	/ˈdɪlɪdʒənt/ 	adj. 勤奋的hit a plateau 	达到平台期slacken off 	松懈pin down 	确定；明确【全文翻译】1924年，美国国家研究委员会派遣了两名工程师到芝加哥附近的一家名为霍桑工厂的电话零部件厂指导一系列实验，希望他们能弄清楚车间照明对工人生产效率的影响。然而，这些研究最终提出了一个极具影响力的概念——“霍桑效应”，这个概念认为，受试行为本身可以改变实验对象的行为。这个观念的提出源于工厂女工们令人困惑的行为。根据实验的描述，当照明增强时，她们的每小时产量上升，但是当照明变暗时，产量也同样上升。实验中做了什么并不重要，只要某些东西有所改变，生产效率就会提高。似乎意识到自己正被作为实验对象这一点本身已足以改变工人们的行为。几十年后，同样的数据被用于计量经济学分析。霍桑实验又带来了另一个令人吃惊的结果。与记录中的描述相反，没有找到系统性的证据表明生产效率的高低与照明变化有关。结果表明，实验的特殊方式可能导致对所发生情况的误导性理解。例如，照明总是在星期日进行变动。当星期一重新开始工作时，产量相比上个星期六会如期上升，并且在接下来的几天里会继续上升。然而，与没有进行实验那几周的数据进行比较表明，每个星期一产量总是会上升。不管在什么情况下，在一周的开始几天，工人们往往会努力工作，然后达到一个平台期，接着逐渐松懈。这表明所谓的“霍桑效应”很难验证。

questions.article:
Bankers have been blaming themselves for their troubles in public. Behind the scenes, they have been taking aim at someone else: the accounting standard-setters. Their rules, moan the banks, have forced them to report enormous losses, and it’s just not fair. These rules say they must value some assets at the price a third party would pay, not the price managers and regulators would like them to fetch.
Unfortunately, banks’ lobbying now seems to be working. The details may be unknowable, but the independence of standard-setters, essential to the proper functioning of capital markets, is being compromised. And, unless banks carry toxic assets at prices that attract buyers, reviving the banking system will be difficult.
After a bruising encounter with Congress, America’s Financial Accounting Standards Board (FASB) rushed through rule changes. These gave banks more freedom to use models to value illiquid assets and more flexibility in recognizing losses on long-term assets in their income statements. Bob Herz, the FASB’s chairman, cried out against those who “question our motives”. Yet bank shares rose and the changes enhance what one lobbying group politely calls “the use of judgment by management”.
European ministers instantly demanded that the International Accounting Standards Board (IASB) do likewise. The IASB says it does not want to act without overall planning, but the pressure to fold when it completes its reconstruction of rules later this year is strong. Charlie McCreevy, a European commissioner, warned the IASB that it did “not live in a political vacuum” but “in the real world” and that Europe could yet develop different rules.
It was banks that were
on the wrong planet
, with accounts that vastly overvalued assets. Today they argue that market prices overstate losses, because they largely reflect the temporary illiquidity of markets, not the likely extent of bad debts. The truth will not be known for years. But banks’ shares trade below their book value, suggesting that investors are skeptical. And dead markets partly reflect the paralysis of banks which will not sell assets for fear of booking losses, yet are reluctant to buy all those supposed bargains.
To get the system working again, losses must be recognized and dealt with. America’s new plan to buy up toxic assets will not work unless banks mark assets to levels which buyers find attractive. Successful markets require independent and even combative standard-setters. The FASB and IASB have been exactly that, cleaning up rules on stock options and pensions, for example, against hostility from special interests. But by giving in to critics now they are inviting pressure to make more concessions.
1. Bankers complained that they were forced to ________.
2. According to the author, the rule changes of the FASB may result in ________.
3. According to Paragraph 4, McCreevy objects to the IASB’s attempt to ________.
4. The author thinks the banks were “on the wrong planet” in that they ________.
5. The author’s attitude towards standard-setters is one of ________.

sub_questions.analysis: 【整体分析】来源：The Economist《经济学人》于2009年4月8日刊登的文章Messenger, shot（信使出击）。主题：本文主要探讨了银行业在面临困境时将责任归咎于自身，同时将矛头指向会计准则制定者的情况。文章指出了银行抱怨会计规定强迫他们报告巨额损失的不公平之处，并讨论了这种游说对会计准则制定者独立性的威胁以及对资本市场正常运行的影响。文章还提到了美国和欧洲在应对银行问题方面采取的不同态度，以及银行对市场价格和资产估值的争议。最后，文章强调了需要承认和处理损失以使银行系统复苏的重要性，以及独立的会计准则制定者对市场成功运作的必要性。结构：【试题解析】【选项释义】Bankers complained that they were forced to ________.	银行家们抱怨说，他们被迫________。A. follow unfavorable asset evaluation rules	A. 遵循不利的资产评估规则B. collect payments from third parties	B. 向第三方收取费用C. cooperate with the price managers	C. 与价格管理人员合作D. reevaluate some of their assets	D. 对他们的一些资产进行重新评估【考查点】事实细节题。【解题思路】根据题干关键词Bankers complained可以定位到文章第一段第三句“各家银行抱怨说，这些规定迫使他们报告巨额损失，这不公平（report enormous losses, and it’s just not fair）”，说明银行家抱怨这些规则对他们不公平，因此A选项“遵循不利的资产评估规则”正确。【干扰项排除】B选项“向第三方收取费用”，由These rules say they must value some assets at the price a third party would pay（这些准则要求他们对某些资产进行估值时使用第三方愿意支付的价格）可知，文章说的是按照第三方愿意支付的价格评估资产，而不是向第三方收取费用，属于偷换概念；C选项“与价格管理人员合作”在文中没有提及，属于无中生有；D选项“对他们的一些资产进行重新评估”，文章只是说这些规则要求按规定评估资产，没有强迫重新评估资产，属于过度推断。

options.B: the revival of the banking system

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】According to the author, the rule changes of the FASB may result in ________. 根据作者的说法，FASB的规则变化可能会导致________。A. the diminishing role of management A. 管理层的作用越来越小B. the revival of the banking system B. 银行系统的复兴C. the banks’ long-term asset losses C. 银行的长期资产损失D. the weakening of its independence D. 其独立性的减弱【考查点】事实细节题。【解题思路】根据题干关键词the rule changes of the FASB可以定位到文章第二段第二句“细节可能无法得知，但会计准则制定者的独立性（the independence of standard-setters）——对资本市场的正常运行至关重要——正在受到损害（is being compromised）”，说明FASB的规则变化损害了其独立性，因此D选项“其独立性的减弱”正确。【干扰项排除】A选项“管理层的作用越来越小”，由the changes enhance what one lobbying group politely calls “the use of judgment by management”（这些变更增强了一个游说团体委婉表达的“管理层对判断力的运用”）可知，管理层的作用增强了，属于反向干扰；B选项“银行系统的复兴”，由And, unless banks carry toxic assets at prices that attract buyers, reviving the banking system will be difficult.（除非银行以能吸引买家的价格出售不良资产，否则复兴银行系统将变得困难。）可知，银行系统的复兴将变得困难，属于反向干扰；C选项“银行的长期资产损失”，由These gave banks more freedom to use models to value illiquid assets and more flexibility in recognizing losses on long-term assets in their income statements.（这些变更使银行在使用模型评估非流动资产时更加自由，并在其损益表中更加灵活地确认长期资产的损失。）可知，变更能导致更加灵活地确认长期资产的损失，而不是导致长期资产损失，属于曲解原文。

options.B: evade the pressure from their peers
options.C: act on their own in rule-setting

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】According to Paragraph 4, McCreevy objects to the IASB’s attempt to ________. 根据第4段，麦克里维反对国际会计准则委员会试图________。A. keep away from political influences A. 远离政治影响B. evade the pressure from their peers B. 躲避来自同行的压力C. act on their own in rule-setting C. 独立制定规则D. take gradual measures in reform D. 在改革中采取渐进的措施【考查点】推理判断题。【解题思路】根据题干关键词McCreevy可以定位到文章第四段最后一句“欧洲委员会的查理•麦克里维警告IASB说，它‘不生活在政治真空中’，而是‘生活在现实世界中’（did “not live in a political vacuum” but “in the real world”），欧洲迟早会制定不同的准则。”，说明麦克里维反对IASB按照自己的意愿制定准则，因此C选项“独立制定规则”正确。【干扰项排除】A选项“远离政治影响”和D选项“在改革中采取渐进的措施”在文中没有提及，属于无中生有；B选项“躲避来自同行的压力”，由European ministers instantly demanded that the International Accounting Standards Board (IASB) do likewise.（欧洲各国部长们立即要求国际会计准则委员会（IASB）采取同样的行动。）可知，IASB的压力来自于欧洲的部长们，而不是其同行，属于曲解原文。

options.B: exaggerated the real value of their assets
options.C: neglected the likely existence of bad debts
options.D: denied booking losses in their sale of assets

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】The author thinks the banks were “on the wrong planet” in that they ________. 作者认为银行“不切实际”，因为他们________。A. misinterpreted market price indicators A. 误解了市场价格指标B. exaggerated the real value of their assets B. 夸大了他们资产的实际价值C. neglected the likely existence of bad debts C. 忽视了可能存在的坏账D. denied booking losses in their sale of assets D. 否认了他们在出售资产时的账面损失【考查点】事实细节题。【解题思路】根据题干关键词on the wrong planet可以定位到文章第五段第一句“银行才是脱离实际的一方，他们的账目严重高估了资产（with accounts that vastly overvalued assets）”，说明银行账目对资产评估过高，因此B选项“夸大了他们资产的实际价值”正确。【干扰项排除】A选项“误解了市场价格指标”，C选项“忽视了可能存在的坏账”和D选项“否认了他们在出售资产时的账面损失”在文中均无法体现，属于无中生有。

【选项释义】The author’s attitude towards standard-setters is one of ________. 作者对标准制定者的态度是：________。A. satisfaction A. 满意B. skepticism B. 怀疑C. objectiveness C. 客观D. sympathy D. 同情【考查点】观点态度题。【解题思路】根据题干关键词standard-setters可以定位到文章最后一段最后一句“但是，他们现在对批评者做出了让步，将面临做出更多让步的压力（they are inviting pressure to make more concessions）”，说明作者认为标准制定者将面临更多压力，对他们持同情态度，因此D选项“同情”正确。【干扰项排除】A选项“满意”，B选项“怀疑”和C选项“客观”在文中均无法体现，属于无中生有。【重点词汇】lobbying 	/ˈlɑːbiɪŋ/ 	n. 游说illiquid 	/ɪˈlɪkwɪd/ 	adj. 非流动的flexibility 	/ˌflɛksəˈbɪləti/ 	n. 灵活性recognition 	/ˌrɛkəɡˈnɪʃən/ 	n. 承认；认可reconstruction 	/ˌriːkənˈstrʌkʃən/ 	n. 重建；改造overstate 	/ˌoʊvərˈsteɪt/ 	v. 夸大；夸张skepticism 	/ˈskɛptɪsɪzəm/ 	n. 怀疑态度；怀疑论paralysis 	/pəˈræləsɪs/ 	n. 瘫痪；麻痹hostile 	/ˈhɑːstl̩/ 	adj. 敌对的；敌意的standard-setters	标准制定者clean up	清理make concessions	做出让步【长难句分析】1. The IASB says it does not want to act without overall planning, but the pressure to fold when it completes its reconstruction of rules later this year is strong.【结构分析】【补充分析】① it does not want to act without overall planning是省略了that的宾语从句，作says的宾语，表达了IASB的观点，即他们不希望在没有总体规划的情况下采取行动；② when引导时间状语从句，表示面临压力的时间，即在国际会计准则委员会今年晚些时候完成规则重建时。【参考译文】IASB表示不希望在没有总体计划之前行动，但等其今年晚些时候完成准则重建时，将会面临巨大的屈服压力。2. America’s new plan to buy up toxic assets will not work unless banks mark assets to levels which buyers find attractive.【结构分析】【补充分析】① unless引导条件状语从句，说明了购买不良资产计划的实施需要满足的前提条件，即银行将资产标记到某种水平；② which引导定语从句，修饰levels，进一步说明了银行标记资产的水平应该是买家认为有吸引力的水平。【参考译文】除非银行将资产定价到买家认为有吸引力的水平，否则美国购买不良资产的新计划将无法实施。【全文翻译】银行家们在公开场合一直将遇到的麻烦归咎于自身。在私底下，他们则将矛头指向别人：会计准则制定者。各家银行抱怨说，这些规定迫使他们报告巨额损失，这不公平。这些准则要求他们对某些资产进行估值时使用第三方愿意支付的价格，而不是管理者和监管机构希望得到的价格。不幸的是，银行的游说似乎产生了效果。细节可能无法得知，但会计准则制定者的独立性——对资本市场的正常运行至关重要——正在受到损害。除非银行以能吸引买家的价格出售不良资产，否则复兴银行系统将变得困难。在与国会的激烈交锋之后，美国财务会计准则委员会（FASB）草草通过了准则变更。这些变更使银行在使用模型评估非流动资产时更加自由，并在其损益表中更加灵活地确认长期资产的损失。FASB的主席鲍勃•赫尔兹对那些“质疑我们动机”的人表示不满。然而，银行股价上涨了，这些变更增强了一个游说团体委婉表达的“管理层对判断力的运用”。欧洲各国部长们立即要求国际会计准则委员会（IASB）采取同样的行动。IASB表示不希望在没有总体计划之前行动，但等其今年晚些时候完成准则重建时，将会面临巨大的屈服压力。欧洲委员会的查理•麦克里维警告IASB说，它“不生活在政治真空中”，而是“生活在现实世界中”，欧洲迟早会制定不同的准则。银行才是脱离实际的一方，他们的账目严重高估了资产。如今他们辩称市场价格夸大了损失，因为这些价格在很大程度上反映了市场暂时的流动性不足，而不是坏账的可能达到的程度。真相将在数年后才能知晓。但银行的股价低于账面价值，表明投资者持怀疑态度。而萧条的市场在一定程度上反映了银行的瘫痪，他们为了避免账面显示亏损不愿意出售资产，但又不愿购买那些所谓的廉价资产。要使系统再次运作起来，必须承认并处理损失。除非银行将资产定价到买家认为有吸引力的水平，否则美国购买不良资产的新计划将无法实施。成功的市场需要独立甚至好斗的会计准则制定者。FASB和IASB一直是这样，比如他们克服了来自特殊利益集团的敌意，对有关股票期权和养老金方面的规则进行了整顿。但是，他们现在对批评者做出了让步，将面临做出更多让步的压力。

questions.article:
In his book
The Tipping Point
, Malcolm Gladwell argues that “social epidemics” are driven in large part by the actions of a tiny minority of special individuals, often called influentials, who are unusually informed, persuasive, or well connected. The idea is intuitively compelling, but it doesn’t explain how ideas actually spread.
The supposed importance of influentials derives from a plausible-sounding but largely untested theory called the “two-step flow of communication”: Information flows from the media to the influentials and from them to everyone else. Marketers have embraced the two-step flow because it suggests that if they can just find and influence the influentials, those select people will do most of the work for them. The theory also seems to explain the sudden and unexpected popularity of certain looks, brands, or neighborhoods. In many such cases, a cursory search for causes finds that some small group of people was wearing, promoting, or developing whatever it is before anyone else paid attention. Anecdotal evidence of this kind fits nicely with the idea that only certain special people can drive trends.
In their recent work, however, some researchers have come up with the finding that influentials have far less impact on social epidemics than is generally supposed. In fact, they don’t seem to be required at all.
The researchers’ argument stems from a simple observation about social influence: With the exception of a few celebrities like Oprah Winfrey—whose outsize presence is primarily a function of media, not interpersonal, influence—even the most influential members of a population simply don’t interact with that many others. Yet it is precisely these non-celebrity influentials who, according to the two-step-flow theory, are supposed to drive social epidemics, by influencing their friends and colleagues directly. For a social epidemic to occur, however, each person so affected must then influence his or her own acquaintances, who must in turn influence theirs, and so on; and just how many others pay attention to each of
these people
has little to do with the initial influential. If people in the network just two degrees removed from the initial influential prove resistant, for example, the cascade of change won’t propagate very far or affect many people.
Building on the basic truth about interpersonal influence, the researchers studied the dynamics of social influence by conducting thousands of computer simulations of populations, manipulating a number of variables relating to people’s ability to influence others and their tendency to be influenced. They found that the principal requirement for
what is called “global cascades”—the widespread propagation of influence through networks—is the presence not of a few influentials but, rather, of a critical mass of easily influenced people.
1. By citing the book
The Tipping Point
, the author intends to ________.
2. The author suggests that the “two-step-flow theory” ________.
3. What the researchers have observed recently shows that ________.
4. The underlined phrase “these people” in Paragraph 4 refers to the ones who ________.
5. What is the essential element in the dynamics of social influence?

options.A: analyze the consequences of social epidemics
options.B: discuss influentials’ function in spreading ideas
options.C: exemplify people’s intuitive response to social epidemics
options.D: describe the essential characteristics of influentials

sub_questions.analysis: 【整体分析】来源：Harvard Business Review《哈佛商业评论》于2007年2月1日刊登的文章The Accidental Influentials（偶然的影响力人士）。主题：本文主要探讨了有影响力的个体在社会潮流中的作用，以及他们是否真的是推动思想传播的关键因素。文章指出，尽管有影响力的人通常被认为对社会流行病有重要影响，但最近的研究表明他们的影响力远不如想象中的重要。相反，易受影响的大众才是社会潮流传播的主要推动力量。通过对人际关系和信息传播进行计算机模拟，研究人员发现，全球级连锁反应发生的关键并不在于少数有影响力的人，而是在于大量易受影响的人的存在。结构：【试题解析】【选项释义】By citing the bookThe Tipping Point, the author intends to ________.	通过引用《引爆点》一书，作者打算________。A. analyze the consequences of social epidemicsA. 分析社会潮流的后果B. discuss influentials’ function in spreading ideasB. 讨论有影响力的人在传播思想方面的作用C. exemplify people’s intuitive response to social epidemicsC. 举例说明人们对社会潮流的直觉反应D. describe the essential characteristics of influentialsD. 描述有影响力的人的基本特征【考查点】推理判断题。【解题思路】根据题干关键词The Tipping Point可以定位到文章第一段第一句“在马尔科姆•格拉德威尔的书《引爆点》中，他认为，‘社会潮流’在很大程度上是由一小部分特殊个体的行动推动的（are driven in large part by the actions of a tiny minority of special individuals）”，接着最后一句说到“这个观点在直觉上很有吸引力，但它并没有解释思想实际上是如何传播的（it doesn’t explain how ideas actually spread）”，说明引用这本书是为了引出对有影响力的人在社会传播中作用的讨论，因此B选项“讨论有影响力的人在传播思想方面的作用”正确。【干扰项排除】A选项“分析社会潮流的后果”在文中没有提及，属于无中生有；C选项“举例说明人们对社会潮流的直觉反应”，由The idea is intuitively compelling（这个观点在直觉上很有吸引力）可知，《引爆点》一书的观点在直觉上令人觉得信服，但这不是作者引用其的目的，属于曲解原文；D选项“描述有影响力的人的基本特征”，由often called influentials, who are unusually informed, persuasive, or well connected（这些个体通常被称为有影响力的人，他们见多识广、能言善辩、或人脉极广）可知，《引爆点》一书中描写了有影响力的人的基本特征，但这不是作者引用其的目的，属于曲解原文。

options.A: serves as a solution to marketing problems
options.B: has helped explain certain prevalent trends
options.D: requires solid evidence for its validity

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】The author suggests that the “two-step-flow theory” ________. 作者认为，“两级流动传播理论”________。A. serves as a solution to marketing problems A. 是一种解决营销问题的方法B. has helped explain certain prevalent trends B. 有助于解释某些盛行的趋势C. has won support from influentials C. 赢得了有影响力的人的支持D. requires solid evidence for its validity D. 需要坚实的证据来证明其有效性【考查点】事实细节题。【解题思路】根据题干关键词two-step-flow theory可以定位到文章第二段第一句“所谓有影响力人士的重要性源自一个听起来很合理但很大程度上未经验证的（a plausible-sounding but largely untested）理论，称为‘两级流动传播’”，说明“两级流动传播理论”还需要证据的验证，因此D选项“需要坚实的证据来证明其有效性”正确。【干扰项排除】A选项“是一种解决营销问题的方法”和C选项“赢得了有影响力的人的支持”在文中没有提及，属于无中生有；B选项“有助于解释某些盛行的趋势”，由The theory also seems to explain the sudden and unexpected popularity of certain looks, brands, or neighborhoods.（这个理论似乎也可以解释某些外观、品牌或社区为何会突然出乎意料地受到追捧。）可知，该理论只是似乎可以解释这些趋势，而不是有助于解释，属于过度推断。

options.A: the power of influence goes with social interactions
options.B: interpersonal links can be enhanced through the media
options.C: influentials have more channels to reach the public
options.D: most celebrities enjoy wide media attention

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】What the researchers have observed recently shows that ________. 研究人员最近观察到的情况表明，________。A. the power of influence goes with social interactions A. 影响力与社会交往相伴而行B. interpersonal links can be enhanced through the media B. 人际联系可以通过媒体得到加强C. influentials have more channels to reach the public C. 有影响力的人有更多的渠道来接触公众D. most celebrities enjoy wide media attention D. 大多数名人享有媒体的广泛关注【考查点】事实细节题。【解题思路】根据题干关键词the researchers have observed可以定位到文章第四段第二句“然而，根据两级流动传播理论，正是这些非名人但有影响力的人通过直接影响他们的朋友和同事（by influencing their friends and colleagues directly）推动了社会潮流”，说明社会潮流的传播是通过普通人之间的交往和影响，因此A选项“影响力与社会交往相伴而行”正确。【干扰项排除】B选项“人际联系可以通过媒体得到加强”和D选项“大多数名人享有媒体的广泛关注”在文中没有提及，属于无中生有；C选项“有影响力的人有更多的渠道来接触公众”，由even the most influential members of a population simply don’t interact with that many others（甚至是群体中最有影响力的成员也不会与那么多人互动）可知，有影响力的人也没有更多的渠道，属于反向干扰。

options.A: stay outside the network of social influence
options.B: have little contact with the source of influence
options.C: are influenced and then influence others
options.D: are influenced by the initial influential

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】The underlined phrase “these people” in Paragraph 4 refers to the ones who ________. 第4段中划线的短语“these people”是指那些________的人。A. stay outside the network of social influence A. 处于社会影响网络之外B. have little contact with the source of influence B. 与影响源接触很少C. are influenced and then influence others C. 被影响然后影响他人D. are influenced by the initial influential D. 被最初的有影响力的人所影响【考查点】词汇推断题。【解题思路】根据题干关键词these people可以定位到文章第四段第三句“然而，对于社会潮流的发生，每个受影响的人必须进而影响自己的熟人，然后再由他们影响他们的熟人，以此类推；而these people能得到多少人关注与最初的有影响力的人关系不大”，说明these people指代前面提到的这一类人，即受到影响又去影响他人的人，因此C选项“被影响然后影响他人”正确。【干扰项排除】A选项“处于社会影响网络之外”，由文章可知这些人处于社会影响网络中，属于反向干扰；B选项“与影响源接触很少”，由how many others pay attention to each of these people has little to do with the initial influential（每个人能得到多少人关注与最初的有影响力的人关系不大）可知，与影响源头接触很少的是关注these people的人，而不是these people本身，属于曲解原文；D选项“被最初的有影响力的人所影响”，由each person so affected must then influence his or her own acquaintances（每个受影响的人必须进而影响自己的熟人）可知，被最初的有影响力的人所影响的人进而影响到的熟人才是these people，属于曲解原文。

options.D: The inclination to rely on others.

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】What is the essential element in the dynamics of social influence? 在推动社会影响的动力中，什么是基本要素？A. The eagerness to be accepted. A. 渴望被接受的心情。B. The impulse to influence others. B. 影响他人的冲动。C. The readiness to be influenced. C. 愿意被影响的准备。D. The inclination to rely on others. D. 依赖他人的倾向。【考查点】事实细节题。【解题思路】根据题干关键词the essential element可以定位到文章最后一段最后一句“他们发现，‘全球级连锁反应’——即影响通过网络广泛传播——形成的主要条件并不是一些有影响力的人，而是大量易受影响的人的存在（the presence not of a few influentials but, rather, of a critical mass of easily influenced people）”，说明基本要素是大量容易受到影响的人，因此C选项“愿意被影响的准备。”正确。【干扰项排除】A选项“渴望被接受的心情。”和D选项“依赖他人的倾向。”在文中没有提及，属于无中生有；B选项“影响他人的冲动。”，由manipulating a number of variables relating to people’s ability to influence others and their tendency to be influenced（操纵与人们影响他人的能力和易受影响性有关的多个变量）可知影响他人的冲动是因素之一，但不是最主要的因素，属于过度推断。【重点词汇】influential 	/ɪnˈfluːɛnʃəl/ 	n. 有影响力的人；有影响力的群体outsize 	/ˌaʊtˈsaɪz/ 	adj. 特大的；超大号的interpersonal 	/ˌɪntərˈpɜːrsənl/ 	adj. 人际的；人与人之间的acquaintance 	/əˈkweɪntəns/ 	n. 熟人；相识的人cascade 	/kæˈskeɪd/ 	n. 级联；级联反应propagate 	/ˈprɑːpəɡeɪt/ 	v. 传播；繁殖resistance 	/rɪˈzɪstəns/ 	n. 抵抗；反抗plausible 	/ˈplɔːzəbl/ 	adj. 貌似可信的；合理的untested 	/ʌnˈtɛstɪd/ 	adj. 未经测试的；未经验证的cursory 	/ˈkɜːrsəri/ 	adj. 粗略的；匆忙的anecdotal 	/ˌænɪkˈdoʊtl/ 	adj. 轶事的；趣闻的two-step flow	两级流动传播social epidemic	社会潮流critical mass	临界质量；临界点【长难句分析】1. Marketers have embraced the two-step flow because it suggests that if they can just find and influence the influentials, those select people will do most of the work for them.【结构分析】【补充分析】① because引导原因状语从句，解释了为什么营销人员会接受两级流动传播理论，即该理论表明了某种观点；② that引导宾语从句，作suggests的宾语，说明该理论表明的内容是这些精选出来的人将会为他们做大部分的工作；③ if引导条件状语从句，提供了一个前提条件，即如果营销人员能够找到并影响有影响力的人，这些精选出来的人就会为他们做大部分工作。【参考译文】营销人员接受了这个两级流动传播，因为它表明，如果他们能找到并影响那些有影响力的人，那么这些精选出来的人就会为他们做大部分工作。2. In many such cases, a cursory search for causes finds that some small group of people was wearing, promoting, or developing whatever it is before anyone else paid attention.【结构分析】【补充分析】① that引导宾语从句，作finds的宾语，表示调查发现的内容是一些小群体的人在穿着、推广或开发某种事物；② whatever引导宾语从句，作was wearing, promoting, or developing的宾语，指代前文提到的某种事物，表示无论是什么事物，不论具体是什么；③ before引导时间状语从句，表示在其他人注意之前，指代的小群体已经开始穿着、推广或开发某种事物。【参考译文】在许多这样的案例中，只需简单调查就能发现原因，在其他人注意到之前，一些小群体正在率先使用、推广或开发某些东西。【全文翻译】在马尔科姆•格拉德威尔的书《引爆点》中，他认为，“社会潮流”在很大程度上是由一小部分特殊个体的行动推动的，这些个体通常被称为有影响力的人，他们见多识广、能言善辩或人脉极广。这个观点在直觉上很有吸引力，但它并没有解释思想实际上是如何传播的。所谓有影响力人士的重要性源自一个听起来很合理但很大程度上未经验证的理论，称为“两级流动传播”：信息从媒体流向有影响力的人，然后再从他们传播给其他人。营销人员接受了这个两级流动传播，因为它表明，如果他们能找到并影响那些有影响力的人，那么这些精选出来的人就会为他们做大部分工作。这个理论似乎也可以解释某些外观、品牌或社区为何会突然出乎意料地受到追捧。在许多这样的案例中，只需简单调查就能发现原因，在其他人注意到之前，一些小群体正在率先使用、推广或开发某些东西。这种坊间传闻的证据与只有某些特殊人物才能推动潮流的观念相吻合。然而，在他们最近的研究中，一些研究人员得出了这样的发现：有影响力的人对社会潮流的影响远远不如人们通常所认为的那样重要。事实上，他们似乎根本不是必需的。研究人员的论点源于关于社交影响的一个简单观察：除了像奥普拉•温弗瑞这样的几位名人——她的超凡影响主要是媒体而不是人际影响的结果——甚至是群体中最有影响力的成员也不会与那么多人互动。然而，根据两级流动传播理论，正是这些非名人但有影响力的人通过直接影响他们的朋友和同事推动了社会潮流。然而，对于社会潮流的发生，每个受影响的人必须进而影响自己的熟人，然后再由他们影响他们的熟人，以此类推；而每个人能得到多少人关注与最初的有影响力的人关系不大。例如，如果在这个人际网络中，那些距离有影响力的人仅两个层级的人不愿受到影响，那么变化的连锁反应就不会传播得很远或影响到很多人。基于人际影响的基本事实，研究人员通过对人群进行数千次的计算机模拟，并操纵与人们影响他人的能力和易受影响性有关的多个变量，研究了社会影响的动态。他们发现，“全球级连锁反应”——即影响通过网络广泛传播——形成的主要条件并不是一些有影响力的人，而是大量易受影响的人的存在。

questions.article:
Over the past decade, thousands of patents have been granted for what are called business methods. Amazon.com received one for its “one-click” online payment system. Merrill Lynch got legal protection for an asset allocation strategy. One inventor patented a technique for lifting a box.
Now the nation’s top patent court appears completely ready to scale back on business-method patents, which have been controversial ever since they were first authorized 10 years ago. In a move that has intellectual-property lawyers abuzz, the U.S. Court of Appeals for the Federal Circuit said it would use a particular case to conduct a broad review of business-method patents.
In re Bilski
, as the case is known, is “a very big deal,” says Dennis D. Crouch of the University of Missouri School of Law. It “has the potential to eliminate an entire class of patents.”
Curbs on business-method claims would be a dramatic
about-face
, because it was the Federal Circuit itself that introduced such patents with its 1998 decision in the so-called State Street Bank case, approving a patent on a way of pooling mutual-fund assets. That ruling produced an explosion in business-method patent filings, initially by emerging Internet companies trying to stake out exclusive rights to specific types of online transactions. Later, more established companies raced to add such patents to their files, if only as a defensive move against rivals that might beat them to the punch. In 2005, IBM noted in a court filing that it had been issued more than 300 business-method patents, despite the fact that it questioned the legal basis for granting them. Similarly, some Wall Street investment firms armed themselves with patents for financial products, even as they took positions in court cases opposing the practice.
The Bilski case involves a claimed patent on a method for hedging risk in the energy market. The Federal Circuit issued an unusual order stating that the case would be heard by all 12 of the court’s judges, rather than a typical panel of three, and that one issue it wants to evaluate is whether it should “reconsider” its State Street Bank ruling.
The Federal Circuit’s action comes in the wake of a series of recent decisions by the Supreme Court that has narrowed the scope of protections for patent holders. Last April, for example, the justices signaled that too many patents were being upheld for “inventions” that are obvious. The judges on the Federal Circuit are “reacting to the anti-patent trend at the Supreme Court,” says Harold C. Wegner, a patent attorney and professor at George Washington University Law School.
1. Business-method patents have recently aroused concern because of ________.
2. Which of the following is true of the Bilski case?
3. The word “about-face” (Para. 3) most probably means ________.
4. We learn from the last two paragraphs that business-method patents ________.
5. Which of the following would be the subject of the text?

options.C: the possible restriction on their granting

sub_questions.analysis: 【整体分析】来源：Bloomberg《彭博资讯》于2008年2月21日刊登的文章A Pending Threat to Patents（对专利的潜在威胁）。主题：本文主要探讨了美国最高专利法院对商业方法专利的限制趋势。文章指出在过去十年间，大量商业方法专利被授予，引发了争议。然而，最高专利法院和联邦巡回上诉法院近期的裁决表明他们倾向于限制商业方法专利的范围。文章提到了具体的案例比尔斯基案，该案将由全部12名法官审理，并可能导致商业方法专利被重新评估。这一行动被视为对最高法院反专利趋势的回应。总之，文章讨论了商业方法专利的争议以及对其限制的可能性。结构：【试题解析】【选项释义】Business-method patents have recently aroused concern because of ________.	商业方法专利最近引起了人们的关注，因为________。A. their limited value to businesses	A. 它们对企业的价值有限B. their connection with asset allocation	B. 它们与资产分配的关系C. the possible restriction on their granting	C. 它们的批准可能受到限制D. the controversy over their authorization	D. 它们的授予引起争议【考查点】事实细节题。【解题思路】根据题干关键词Business-method patents可以定位到文章第二段第一句“现在，美国最高专利法院似乎完全准备限制商业方法专利（scale back on business-method patents）”，这是一个“让知识产权律师们议论纷纷的举动（has intellectual-property lawyers abuzz）”，说明商业方法专利引起人们关注的原因是法院准备限制其授予，因此C选项“它们的批准可能受到限制”正确。【干扰项排除】A选项“它们对企业的价值有限”，由Over the past decade, thousands of patents have been granted for what are called business methods.（过去十年间，已经授予了成千上万项所谓商业方法的专利。）可知，商业方法专利对企业很有价值，属于反向干扰；B选项“它们与资产分配的关系”，由Merrill Lynch got legal protection for an asset allocation strategy.（美林证券获得了资产配置策略的法律保护。）可知，资产分配的商业方法专利只是为了举例说明授予的数量之多，与引起人们关注的原因无关，属于曲解原文；D选项“它们的授予引起争议”，由which have been controversial ever since they were first authorized 10 years ago（这些专利自从十年前首次获得授权以来一直备受争议）可知，商业方法专利一直以来就有争议，与最近引起人们关注无关，属于曲解原文。

options.A: Its ruling complies with the court decisions.
options.B: It involves a very big business transaction.
options.C: It has been dismissed by the Federal Circuit.
options.D: It may change the legal practices in the U.S.

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】Which of the following is true of the Bilski case? 以下哪项是比尔斯基案的真实情况？A. Its ruling complies with the court decisions. A. 其裁决符合法院的决定。B. It involves a very big business transaction. B. 它涉及一个非常大的商业交易。C. It has been dismissed by the Federal Circuit. C. 它已被联邦巡回法院驳回。D. It may change the legal practices in the U.S. D. 它可能会改变美国的法律惯例。【考查点】推理判断题。【解题思路】根据题干关键词Bilski case可以定位到文章第二段最后一句“它‘有可能消除整一个专利类别（eliminate an entire class of patents）’”，说明比尔斯基案可能会导致美国法院未来对专利法律惯例方面的改变，因此D选项“它可能会改变美国的法律惯例。”正确。【干扰项排除】A选项“其裁决符合法院的决定。”在文中没有提及，属于无中生有；B选项“它涉及一个非常大的商业交易。”，由In re Bilski, as the case is known, is “a very big deal”（比尔斯基案这个大家都知道的案子“事关重大”）可知，文章是说这个案子很重大，而不是指大的交易，属于曲解原文；C选项“它已被联邦巡回法院驳回。”，由the U.S. Court of Appeals for the Federal Circuit said it would use a particular case to conduct a broad review of business-method patents（美国联邦巡回上诉法院表示将利用一起特别案例对商业方法专利进行广泛审查）可知，法院是通过这个案子对商业方法专利进行审查，不是要驳回此案，属于曲解原文。

【选项释义】The word “about-face” (Para. 3) most probably means ________. “about-face”（第3段）这个词最有可能意味着________。A. loss of good will A. 善意的丧失B. increase of hostility B. 敌意的增加C. change of attitude C. 态度的改变D. enhancement of dignity D. 尊严的增强【考查点】词汇推断题。【解题思路】根据题干关键词about-face可以定位到第三段第一句“对商业方法专利的限制将是一个重大about-face，因为在1998年，正是联邦巡回上诉法院自己在判决名为道富银行案时引入了这样的专利”，上文说到法院准备限制商业方法专利，但最初正是法院自己引入了这样的专利，说明这是一个改变，因此C选项“态度的改变”正确。【干扰项排除】A选项“善意的丧失”，B选项“敌意的增加”和D选项“尊严的增强”在文中均无法体现，属于无中生有。

options.C: lower the esteem for patent holders

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】We learn from the last two paragraphs that business-method patents ________. 我们从最后两段得知，商业方法专利________。A. are immune to legal challenges A. 不受法律挑战的影响B. are often unnecessarily issued B. 往往是不必要地授予的C. lower the esteem for patent holders C. 降低了对专利持有者的尊重D. increase the incidence of risks D. 增加了风险的发生率【考查点】事实细节题。【解题思路】根据题干关键词the last two paragraphs可以定位到文章最后一段第二句“例如，去年四月，法官们表示太多专利被授予给了平淡无奇的‘发明’（too many patents were being upheld for “inventions” that are obvious）”，说明很多商业方法专利的授予是没有必要的，因此B选项“往往是不必要地授予的”正确。【干扰项排除】A选项“不受法律挑战的影响”，C选项“降低了对专利持有者的尊重”和D选项“增加了风险的发生率”在文中均没有提及，属于无中生有。

options.A: A looming threat to business-method patents.
options.C: A legal case regarding business-method patents.
options.D: A prevailing trend against business-method patents.

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】Which of the following would be the subject of the text? 以下哪项是本文的主题？A. A looming threat to business-method patents. A. 对商业方法专利的一个潜在的威胁。B. Protection for business-method patent holders. B. 对商业方法专利持有者的保护。C. A legal case regarding business-method patents. C. 一个关于商业方法专利的法律案例。D. A prevailing trend against business-method patents. D. 一个反对商业方法专利的普遍趋势。【考查点】主旨大意题。【解题思路】文章主要讨论了美国最高专利法院和联邦巡回上诉法院对商业方法专利进行限制和审查的动态。文章提到了近期的裁决限制了专利持有人的保护范围，特别提到了比尔斯基案作为一个重要的案例，有可能消除整个商业方法专利类别。由此可知，文章的主题可以被概括为商业方法专利面临威胁和限制，因此A选项“对商业方法专利的一个潜在的威胁。”正确。【干扰项排除】B选项“对商业方法专利持有者的保护。”在文中没有提及，属于无中生有；C选项“一个关于商业方法专利的法律案例。”只在文章的某部分提及，不能概括全文的主旨大意，属于以偏概全；D选项“一个反对商业方法专利的普遍趋势。”，由reacting to the anti-patent trend at the Supreme Court（回应最高法院的反专利趋势）可知，目前只有最高法院有反商业方法专利的趋势，还没有到普遍的程度，属于过度推断。【重点词汇】controversy 	/ˈkɒntrəvɜːsi/ 	n. 争议；争论authorized 	/ˈɔːθəraɪzd/ 	adj. 被授权的；合法的patent 	/ˈpætnt/ 	n. 专利；专利权exclusive 	/ɪkˈskluːsɪv/ 	adj. 独占的；专有的hedging 	/ˈhɛdʒɪŋ/ 	n. 对冲；保值explosion 	/ɪkˈspləʊʒən/ 	n. 爆炸；激增panel 	/ˈpænəl/ 	n. 小组；座谈小组upheld 	/ʌpˈhɛld/ 	v. 维持；支持prevailing 	/prɪˈveɪlɪŋ/ 	adj. 流行的；普遍的intellectual-property	知识产权defensive move 		防御性举措scale back 		减少；缩减stake out 		标榜；宣称beat someone to the punch 	抢先一步be armed with 		装备有；凭借in the wake of 		在……之后【长难句分析】1. In a move that has intellectual-property lawyers abuzz, the U.S. Court of Appeals for the Federal Circuit said it would use a particular case to conduct a broad review of business-method patents.【结构分析】【补充分析】① that引导定语从句，修饰move，描述这个行动引起了知识产权律师的激烈讨论；② it would use a particular case to conduct a broad review of business-method patents是省略了that的宾语从句，作said的宾语，解释了法院的行动，即他们计划使用一个特定的案例来进行商业方法专利的广泛审查。【参考译文】在一个让知识产权律师们议论纷纷的举动中，美国联邦巡回上诉法院表示将利用一起特别案例对商业方法专利进行广泛审查。2. In 2005, IBM noted in a court filing that it had been issued more than 300 business-method patents, despite the fact that it questioned the legal basis for granting them.【结构分析】【补充分析】① that引导宾语从句，作noted的宾语，解释了IBM在法庭文件中提到的内容，即IBM已经获得了300多项商业方法专利；② that引导同位语从句，作fact的同位语，进一步说明IBM对这些专利的法律依据持怀疑态度的事实。【参考译文】2005年，IBM在法庭文件中指出，尽管对授予这些专利的法律依据表示质疑，但它已获得了300多项商业方法专利。【全文翻译】过去十年间，已经授予了成千上万项所谓商业方法的专利。亚马逊公司获得了其“一键式”在线支付系统的专利。美林证券获得了资产配置策略的法律保护。有人申请了一个提箱技术的专利。现在，美国最高专利法院似乎完全准备限制商业方法专利，这些专利自从十年前首次获得授权以来一直备受争议。在一个让知识产权律师们议论纷纷的举动中，美国联邦巡回上诉法院表示将利用一起特别案例对商业方法专利进行广泛审查。比尔斯基案这个大家都知道的案子“事关重大”，密苏里大学法学院的丹尼斯•D.克劳奇说。它“有可能消除一整个专利类别”。对商业方法专利的限制将是一个重大转变，因为在1998年，正是联邦巡回上诉法院自己在判决名为道富银行案时引入了这样的专利，批准了一项关于共同基金资产筹集方法的专利。该裁决引发了商业方法专利申请的激增，最初是由新兴互联网公司试图确保对特定在线交易类型的专有权。后来，更多老牌公司争相将这类专利收入囊中，即使只是为了防范可能先下手为强的竞争对手的防御型举措。2005年，IBM在法庭文件中指出，尽管对授予这些专利的法律依据表示质疑，但它已获得了300多项商业方法专利。同样，一些华尔街投资公司也通过申请金融产品专利来武装自己，即使他们在诉讼案件中对这一做法持反对态度。比尔斯基案涉及一项关于能源市场风险对冲方法的专利申请。联邦巡回上诉法院发布了一项不寻常的命令，宣布该案将由法院的所有12名法官审理，而不是通常的三人小组，另外它希望评估的一个问题是是否应该“重新审议”道富银行裁决。联邦巡回上诉法院的行动是在最高法院最近一系列裁决之后采取的，这些裁决已经限制了专利持有人的保护范围。例如，去年四月，法官们表示太多专利被授予给了平淡无奇的“发明”。专利律师兼乔治•华盛顿大学法学院教授哈罗德•C.韦格纳表示，联邦巡回上诉法院的法官们正在“回应最高法院的反专利趋势”。

questions.article:
Of all the changes that have taken place in English-language newspapers during the past quarter-century, perhaps the most far-reaching has been the inexorable decline in the scope and seriousness of their arts coverage.
It is difficult to the point of impossibility for the average reader under the age of forty to imagine a time when high-quality arts criticism could be found in most big-city newspapers. Yet a considerable number of the most significant collections of criticism published in the 20th century consisted in large part of newspaper reviews. To read such books today is to marvel at the fact that their learned contents were once deemed suitable for publication in general-circulation dailies.
We are even farther removed from the unfocused newspaper reviews published in England between the turn of the 20th century and the eve of World War II, at a time when newsprint was dirt-cheap and stylish arts criticism was considered an ornament to the publications in which it appeared. In those far-off days, it was taken for granted that the critics of major papers would write in detail and at length about the events they covered. Theirs was a serious business, and even those reviewers who wore their learning lightly, like George Bernard Shaw and Ernest Newman, could be trusted to know what they were about. These men believed in journalism as a calling, and were proud to be published in the daily press. “So few authors have brains enough or literary gift enough to keep their own end up in journalism,” Newman wrote, “that I am tempted to define ‘journalism’ as ‘a term of contempt applied by writers who are not read to writers who are’.”
Unfortunately, these critics are virtually forgotten. Neville Cardus, who wrote for the
Manchester Guardian
from 1917 until shortly before his death in 1975, is now known solely as a writer of essays on the game of cricket. During his lifetime, though, he was also one of England’s foremost classical-music critics, and a stylist so widely admired that his
Autobiography
(1947) became a best-seller. He was knighted in 1967, the first music critic to be so honored. Yet only one of his books is now in print, and his vast body of writings on music is unknown save to specialists.
Is there any chance that Cardus’s criticism will enjoy a revival? The prospect seems remote. Journalistic tastes had changed long before his death, and postmodern readers have little use for the richly upholstered Vicwardian prose in which he specialized. Moreover, the amateur tradition in music criticism has been in headlong retreat.
1. It is indicated in Paragraphs 1 and 2 that ________.
2. Newspaper reviews in England before World War II were characterized by ________.
3. Which of the following would Shaw and Newman most probably agree on?
4. What can be learned about Cardus according to the last two paragraphs?
5. What would be the best title for the text?

options.A: arts criticism has disappeared from big-city newspapers
options.B: English-language newspapers used to carry more arts reviews
options.C: high-quality newspapers retain a large body of readers
options.D: young readers doubt the suitability of criticism on dailies

sub_questions.analysis: 【整体分析】来源：Commentary《评论》于2007年11月1日刊登的文章The Amateur as Critic（业余评论家）。主题：本文主要探讨了过去25年英语报纸发生的变化，特别是艺术报道范围和严肃性的下降。它指出了过去报纸上高质量艺术评论的存在，以及20世纪初至二战前夕英国报纸上的评论风格。文章提到了被遗忘的评论家内维尔•卡德斯及其对音乐的评论，以及他们的作品如今的情况和前景。最后，文章指出了新闻业口味的变化和音乐评论中业余传统的衰退。结构：【试题解析】【选项释义】It is indicated in Paragraphs 1 and 2 that ________.	第1段和第2段中指出：________。A. arts criticism has disappeared from big-city newspapers	A. 艺术批评已经从大城市的报纸上消失了B. English-language newspapers used to carry more arts reviews	B. 英语报纸曾经刊登过更多的艺术评论C. high-quality newspapers retain a large body of readers	C. 高质量的报纸保留了大量的读者D. young readers doubt the suitability of criticism on dailies	D. 年轻的读者对日报上的评论文章是否合适表示怀疑【考查点】事实细节题。【解题思路】根据题干关键词Paragraphs 1 and 2可以定位到文章第一段“最具深远影响的或许是它们艺术报道的范围和严肃性的不可逆转的下降（the inexorable decline in the scope and seriousness of their arts coverage）”和第二段第一、二句“对于40岁以下的普通读者来说，很难想象过去高质量的艺术评论可以在大城市的报纸上读到（high-quality arts criticism could be found in most big-city newspapers）的时代。然而，在20世纪出版的一系列最重要的评论集中，很大一部分是由报纸评论组成的（consisted in large part of newspaper reviews）”，说明在过去，艺术评论在报纸上是很常见的，因此B选项“英语报纸曾经刊登过更多的艺术评论”正确。【干扰项排除】A选项“艺术批评已经从大城市的报纸上消失了”，由perhaps the most far-reaching has been the inexorable decline in the scope and seriousness of their arts coverage（最具深远影响的或许是它们艺术报道的范围和严肃性的不可逆转的下降）可知，艺术批评只是数量减少，而不是完全消失，属于过度推断；C选项“高质量的报纸保留了大量的读者”在文中没有提及，属于无中生有；D选项“年轻的读者对日报上的评论文章是否合适表示怀疑”，由It is difficult to the point of impossibility for the average reader under the age of forty to imagine a time when high-quality arts criticism could be found in most big-city newspapers.（对于40岁以下的普通读者来说，很难想象过去高质量的艺术评论可以在大城市的报纸上读到的时代。）可知，年轻的读者是难以想象过去在报纸上可以读到评论文章，而不是对这种做法感到怀疑，属于曲解原文。

【选项释义】Newspaper reviews in England before World War II were characterized by ________. 二战前英国的报纸评论的特点是________。A. free themes A. 自由的主题B. casual style B. 随意的风格C. elaborate layout C. 精心设计的版面D. radical viewpoints D. 激进的观点【考查点】事实细节题。【解题思路】根据题干关键词before World War II可以定位到文章第三段第一句“我们与20世纪初期至二战前夕英国报刊上那些主题自由的评论（the unfocused newspaper reviews published in England between the turn of the 20th century and the eve of World War II）相去甚远”，说明二战前英国的报纸评论的主题是很自由的，因此A选项“自由的主题”正确。【干扰项排除】B选项“随意的风格”，C选项“精心设计的版面”和D选项“激进的观点”在文中均没有提及，属于无中生有。

options.A: It is writers’ duty to fulfill journalistic goals.
options.B: It is contemptible for writers to be journalists.
options.C: Writers are likely to be tempted into journalism.
options.D: Not all writers are capable of journalistic writing.

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】Which of the following would Shaw and Newman most probably agree on? 肖和纽曼最可能在以下哪项上达成一致？A. It is writers’ duty to fulfill journalistic goals. A. 完成新闻目标是作家的职责。B. It is contemptible for writers to be journalists. B. 作家当记者是可鄙的。C. Writers are likely to be tempted into journalism. C. 作家有可能被吸引去做新闻工作。D. Not all writers are capable of journalistic writing. D. 不是所有的作家都有能力进行新闻写作。【考查点】推理判断题。【解题思路】根据题干关键词Shaw and Newman可以定位到文章第三段第三、四、五句“他们从事的是一项严肃的事业，即使像乔治•伯纳德•肖和欧内斯特•纽曼这样轻松地展示自己的学识的评论家也能够被信任，他们知道自己的职责（know what they were about）。这些人相信新闻事业是一种使命（believed in journalism as a calling），并为在日报上发表感到自豪。‘有足够的智慧或文学天赋来在新闻事业中站稳脚跟的作家是如此之少（So few authors have brains enough or literary gift enough to keep their own end up in journalism），’纽曼写道”，说明这两人最有可能赞同的观点是并不是所有作家都能胜任新闻写作。因此D选项“不是所有的作家都有能力进行新闻写作。”正确。【干扰项排除】A选项“完成新闻目标是作家的职责。”和C选项“作家有可能被吸引去做新闻工作。”在文中没有提及，属于无中生有；B选项“作家当记者是可鄙的。”，由I am tempted to define ‘journalism’ as ‘a term of contempt applied by writers who are not read to writers who are’（以至于我忍不住要将‘新闻事业’定义为‘不受欢迎的作家对受到欢迎的作家的蔑称’）可知，新闻事业是对那些受到欢迎的作家的轻蔑之词，而不是说作家不屑于成为记者，属于偷换概念。

options.A: His music criticism may not appeal to readers today.
options.B: His reputation as a music critic has long been in dispute.
options.C: His style caters largely to modern specialists.
options.D: His writings fail to follow the amateur tradition.

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】What can be learned about Cardus according to the last two paragraphs? 根据最后两段，可以了解到关于卡德斯的什么？A. His music criticism may not appeal to readers today. A. 他的音乐评论可能对今天的读者没有吸引力。B. His reputation as a music critic has long been in dispute. B. 他作为一个音乐评论家的声誉长期以来一直存在争议。C. His style caters largely to modern specialists.C. 他的风格在很大程度上迎合了现代的专家。D. His writings fail to follow the amateur tradition. D. 他的著作未能遵循业余的传统。【考查点】事实细节题。【解题思路】根据题干关键词Cardus可以定位到文章第四段最后一句“然而，现在只有一本他的书还在出版（only one of his books is now in print）”和第五段第三句“在他去世之前的很长一段时间，新闻业的品味已经发生了变化，后现代读者对他擅长的那种辞藻华丽的维多利亚—爱德华时代风格的散文没有太多兴趣（postmodern readers have little use for the richly upholstered Vicwardian prose in which he specialized）”，说明卡德斯的作品在如今已经失去吸引力，因此A选项“他的音乐评论可能对今天的读者没有吸引力。”正确。【干扰项排除】B选项“他作为一个音乐评论家的声誉长期以来一直存在争议。”，由he was also one of England’s foremost classical-music critics, and a stylist so widely admired（他也是英国最优秀的古典音乐评论家之一，他的文风广受赞赏）可知，卡德斯的声誉一直非常好，属于反向干扰；C选项“他的风格在很大程度上迎合了现代的专家。”，由his vast body of writings on music is unknown save to specialists（他关于音乐的大量著作只有专家才知道）可知，是只有专家知道他的著作，而不是他去迎合专家，属于曲解原文；D选项“他的著作未能遵循业余的传统。”在文中没有提及，属于无中生有。

options.A: Newspapers of the Good Old Days

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】What would be the best title for the text? 本文的最佳标题是什么？A. Newspapers of the Good Old Days A. 报纸过去的黄金岁月B. The Lost Horizon in Newspapers B. 报纸上消失的地平线C. Mournful Decline of Journalism C. 新闻业令人痛心的衰落D. Prominent Critics in Memory D. 记忆中的著名评论家【考查点】主旨大意题。【解题思路】文章讨论了英语报纸艺术报道范围和严肃性的不可逆转的下降，描述了过去高质量艺术评论在大城市报纸上的存在，以及重要评论集中大部分由报纸评论组成的时代。然而，随着时间的推移，新闻业的品味发生了变化，读者对维多利亚—爱德华时代风格的辞藻华丽的散文和音乐评论的业余传统兴趣减少。由此可知，本文是围绕着艺术评论在报纸上的衰退展开的，因此B选项“报纸上消失的地平线”正确。【干扰项排除】A选项“报纸过去的黄金岁月”在文中没有提及，属于无中生有；C选项“新闻业令人痛心的衰落”，文章只讲了艺术评论这个板块的衰落，而不是整个新闻业，属于过度推断；D选项“记忆中的著名评论家”只在文章的某部分提及，不能概括全文的主旨大意，属于以偏概全。【重点词汇】inexorable 	/ɪnˈɛksərəbl/ 	adj. 不可阻挡的coverage 	/ˈkʌvərɪdʒ/ 	n. 覆盖；报道criticism 	/ˈkrɪtɪsɪzəm/ 	n. 批评；评论collection 	/kəˈlɛkʃən/ 	n. 收藏；集合circulation 	/ˌsɜːrkjuˈleɪʃən/ 	n. 发行量；流通unfocused 	/ʌnˈfoʊkəst/ 	adj. 不集中的；散漫的newsprint 	/ˈnjuːzprɪnt/ 	n. 新闻纸ornament 	/ˈɔːrnəmənt/ 	n. 装饰品publication 	/ˌpʌblɪˈkeɪʃən/	n. 出版物length 	/lɛŋθ/ 	n. 长度；篇幅reviewer 	/rɪˈvjuːər/ 	n. 评论家；审查者calling 	/ˈkɔːlɪŋ/ 	n. 使命；职业contempt 	/kənˈtɛmpt/ 	n. 轻视；鄙视specialist 	/ˈspɛʃəlɪst/ 	n. 专家；专业人士upholstered 	/ʌpˈhoʊlstərd/ 	adj. 有填料的；有装饰物的far-reaching	影响深远的high-quality 	高质量的dirt-cheap 	非常便宜的taken for granted 	理所当然【长难句分析】1. We are even farther removed from the unfocused newspaper reviews published in England between the turn of the 20th century and the eve of World War II, at a time when newsprint was dirt-cheap and stylish arts criticism was considered an ornament to the publications in which it appeared.【结构分析】【补充分析】① when引导定语从句，修饰a time，描述了当时的情况，即新闻纸价格便宜和时尚艺术评论被视为出现在报刊上的点缀品；② in which引导定语从句，修饰publications，指出那些时尚艺术评论出现的地方。【参考译文】我们与20世纪初期至二战前夕英国报刊上那些主题自由的评论相去甚远，当时新闻纸张非常便宜，高雅的艺术评论被视为出现在报纸上的点缀品。2. Theirs was a serious business, and even those reviewers who wore their learning lightly, like George Bernard Shaw and Ernest Newman, could be trusted to know what they were about.【结构分析】【补充分析】① who引导定语从句，修饰reviewers，用于描述那些评论家是能够轻松展示自己学识的；② what引导宾语从句，作know的宾语，解释了评论家知道的内容是自己该做什么。【参考译文】他们从事的是一项严肃的事业，即使像乔治•伯纳德•肖和欧内斯特•纽曼这样轻松地展示自己的学识的评论家也能够被信任，他们知道自己的职责。【全文翻译】在过去的25年里，英语报纸发生的所有变化中，最具深远影响的或许是它们艺术报道的范围和严肃性的不可逆转的下降。对于40岁以下的普通读者来说，很难想象过去高质量的艺术评论可以在大城市的报纸上读到的时代。然而，在20世纪出版的一系列最重要的评论集中，很大一部分是由报纸评论组成的。今天阅读这些书籍，我们会惊叹于一个事实：它们的学术内容曾经被认为适合在大众报纸上发表。我们与20世纪初期至二战前夕英国报刊上那些主题自由的评论相去甚远，当时新闻纸张非常便宜，高雅的艺术评论被视为出现在报纸上的点缀品。在那个遥远的时代，人们默认各大报纸的评论家会详细地评论他们所报道的事件。他们从事的是一项严肃的事业，即使像乔治•伯纳德•肖和欧内斯特•纽曼这样轻松地展示自己的学识的评论家也能够被信任，他们知道自己的职责。这些人相信新闻事业是一种使命，并为在日报上发表感到自豪。“有足够的智慧或文学天赋来在新闻事业中站稳脚跟的作家是如此之少，”纽曼写道，“以至于我忍不住要将‘新闻事业’定义为‘不受欢迎的作家对受到欢迎的作家的蔑称’。”不幸的是，这些评论家几乎被遗忘了。内维尔•卡德斯是曾于1917年起为《曼彻斯特卫报》撰稿的评论家，直至他于1975年去世。如今，他仅以撰写关于板球比赛的评论而闻名。然而，在他的一生中，他也是英国最优秀的古典音乐评论家之一，他的文风广受赞赏，以至于他的自传《回忆录》（1947年）成为畅销书。他于1967年被封为爵士，成为首位获此殊荣的音乐评论家。然而，现在只有一本他的书还在出版，他关于音乐的大量著作只有专家才知道。卡德斯的评论是否有机会复苏？前景似乎渺茫。在他去世之前的很长一段时间，新闻业的品味已经发生了变化，后现代读者对他擅长的那种辞藻华丽的维多利亚—爱德华时代风格的散文没有太多兴趣。此外，音乐评论中的业余传统已经急剧衰退。

questions.article:
Directions: The following paragraphs are given in a wrong order. For questions 1-5, you are required to reorganize these paragraphs into a coherent text by choosing from the list A-G and filling them into the numbered boxes. Paragraphs E have been correctly placed. Mark your answers on ANSWER SHEET. (10 points)
[A] The first and more important is the consumer’s growing preference for eating out: the consumption of food and drink in places other than homes has risen from about 32 percent of total consumption in 1995 to 35 percent in 2000 and is expected to approach 38 percent by 2005. This development is boosting wholesale demand from the food service segment by 4 to 5 percent a year across Europe, compared with growth in retail demand of 1 to 2 percent. Meanwhile, as the recession is looming large, people are getting anxious. They tend to keep a tighter hold on their purse and consider eating at home a realistic alternative.
[B] Retail sales of food and drink in Europe’s largest markets are at a standstill, leaving European grocery retailers hungry for opportunities to grow. Most leading retailers have already tried e-commerce, with limited success, and expansion abroad. But almost all have ignored the big, profitable opportunity in their own backyard: the wholesale food and drink trade, which appears to be just the kind of market retailers need.
[C] Will such variations bring about a change in the overall structure of the food and drink market? Definitely not. The functioning of the market is based on flexible trends dominated by potential buyers. In other words, it is up to the buyer, rather than the seller, to decide what to buy. At any rate, this change will ultimately be acclaimed by an ever-growing number of both domestic and international consumers, regardless of how long the current consumer pattern will take hold.
[D] All in all, this clearly seems to be a market in which big retailers could profitably apply their gigantic scale, existing infrastructure, and proven skills in the management of product ranges, logistics, and marketing intelligence. Retailers that master the intricacies of wholesaling in Europe may well expect to rake in substantial profits thereby. At least, that is how it looks as a whole. Closer inspection reveals important differences among the biggest national markets, especially in their customer segments and wholesale structures, as well as the competitive dynamics of individual food and drink categories. Big retailers must understand these differences before they can identify the segments of European wholesaling in which their particular abilities might unseat smaller but entrenched competitors. New skills and unfamiliar business models are needed too.
[E] Despite variations in detail, wholesale markets in the countries that have been closely examined—France, Germany, Italy, and Spain—are made out of the same building blocks. Demand comes mainly from two sources: independent mom-and-pop grocery stores which, unlike large retail chains, are too small to buy straight from producers, and food service operators that cater to consumers when they don’t eat at home. Such food service operators range from snack machines to large institutional catering ventures, but most of these businesses are known in the trade as “horeca”: hotels, restaurants, and cafes. Overall, Europe’s wholesale market for food and drink is growing at the same sluggish pace as the retail market, but the figures, when added together, mask two opposing trends.
[F] For example, wholesale food and drink sales came to $268 billion in France, Germany, Italy, Spain, and the United Kingdom in 2000—more than 40 percent of retail sales. Moreover, average overall margins are higher in wholesale than in retail; wholesale demand from the food service sector is growing quickly as more Europeans eat out more often; and changes in the competitive dynamics of this fragmented industry are at last making it feasible for wholesalers to consolidate.
[G] However, none of these requirements should deter large retailers (and even some large food producers and existing wholesalers) from trying their hand, for those that master the intricacies of wholesaling in Europe stand to reap considerable gains.
1. ________→2. ________→3. ________→4. ________→E→5. ________

One basic weakness in a conservation system based wholly on economic motives is that most members of the land community have no economic value. Yet these creatures are members of the biotic community and, if its stability depends on its integrity, they are entitled to continuance.
When one of these noneconomic categories is threatened and, if we happen to love it, we invent excuses to give it economic importance. At the beginning of the century songbirds were supposed to be disappearing. (1)
Scientists jumped to the rescue with some distinctly shaky evidence to the effect that insects would eat us up if birds failed to control them
. The evidence had to be economic in order to be valid.
It is painful to read these roundabout accounts today. We have no land ethic yet, (2)
but we have at least drawn nearer the point of admitting that birds should continue as a matter of intrinsic right, regardless of the presence or absence of economic advantage to us
.
A parallel situation exists in respect of predatory mammals and fish-eating birds. (3)
Time was when biologists somewhat overworked the evidence that these creatures preserve the health of game by killing the physically weak, or that they prey only on “worthless” species.
Here again, the evidence had to be economic in order to be valid. It is only in recent years that we hear the more honest argument that predators are members of the community, and that no special interest has the right to exterminate them for the sake of a benefit, real or fancied, to itself.
Some species of trees have been “read out of the party” by economics-minded foresters because they grow too slowly, or have too low a sale value to pay as timber crops. (4)
In Europe, where forestry is ecologically more advanced, the noncommercial tree species are recognized as members of the native forest community, to be preserved as such, within reason
. Moreover, some have been found to have a valuable function in building up soil fertility. The interdependence of the forest and its constituent tree species, ground flora, and fauna is taken for granted.
To sum up: a system of conservation based solely on economic self-interest is hopelessly lopsided. (5)
It tends to ignore, and thus eventually to eliminate, many elements in the land community that lack commercial value, but that are essential to its healthy functioning
. It assumes, falsely, that the economic parts of the biotic clock will function without the uneconomic parts.

Directions: Write an essay of 160-200 words based on the picture below. In your essay, you should
1) describe the picture briefly,
2) interpret the implied meaning, and
3) give your comments.
You should write neatly on the ANSWER SHEET. (20 points)

Directions: You are supposed to write for the Postgraduates’ Association a notice to recruit volunteers for an international conference on globalization. The notice should include the basic qualifications for applicants and the other information which you think is relevant.
You should write about 100 words on ANSWER SHEET.
Do not sign your own name at the end of the letter. Use “Postgraduates’ Association” instead. (10 points)

In 1924 America's National Research Council sent two engineers to supervise a series of industrial experiments at a large telephone-parts factory called the Hawthorne Plant near Chicago. It hoped they would learn how stop-floor lighting （1）workers' productivity. Instead, the studies ended （2） giving their name to the "Hawthorne effect", the extremely influential idea that the very （3） to being experimented upon changed subjects' behavior.
The idea arose because of the behavior of the women in the （4） Hawthorne plant. According to （5） of the experiments, their hourly output rose when lighting was increased, but also when it was dimmed. It did not （6） what was done in the experiment; （7）something was changed, productivity rose. A(n) （8） that they were being experimented upon seemed to be （9） to alter workers' behavior （10） itself.
After several decades, the same data were （11） to econometric the analysis. Hawthorne experiments has another surprise in store （12） the descriptions on record, no systematic （13） was found that levels of productivity were related to changes in lighting.
It turns out that peculiar way of conducting the experiments 
may have led to （14） interpretation of what happened. （15）, lighting was always changed on a Sunday. When work started again on Monday, output （16） rose compared with the previous Saturday and （17） to rise for the next couple of days. （18）, a comparison with data for weeks when there was no experimentation showed that output always went up on Monday, workers （19） to be diligent for the first few days of the week in any case, before （20） a plateau and then slackening off. This suggests that the alleged "Hawthorne effect" is hard to pin down.

Of all the changes that have taken place in English-language newspapers during the past quarter-century, perhaps the most far-reaching has been the inexorable decline in the scope and seriousness of their arts coverage.
It is difficult to the point of impossibility for the average reader under the age of forty to imagine a time when high-quality arts criticism could be found in most big-city newspapers. Yet a considerable number of the most significant collections of criticism published in the 20th century consisted in large part of newspaper reviews. To read such books today is to marvel at the fact that their learned contents were once deemed suitable for publication in general-circulation dailies.
We are even farther removed from the unfocused newspaper reviews published in England between the turn of the 20th century and the eve of World War II, at a time when newsprint was dirt-cheap and stylish arts criticism was considered an ornament to the publications in which it appeared. In those far-off days, it was taken for granted that the critics of major papers would write in detail and at length about the events they covered. Theirs was a serious business, and even those reviewers who wore their learning lightly, like George Bernard Shaw and Ernest Newman, could be trusted to know what they were about. These men believed in journalism as a calling, and were proud to be published in the daily press. “So few authors have brains enough or literary gift enough to keep their own end up in journalism,” Newman wrote, “that I am tempted to define ‘journalism’ as ‘a term of contempt applied by writers who are not read to writers who are’.”
Unfortunately, these critics are virtually forgotten. Neville Cardus, who wrote for the Manchester Guardian from 1917 until shortly before his death in 1975, is now known solely as a writer of essays on the game of cricket. During his lifetime, though, he was also one of England's foremost classical-music critics, a stylist so widely admired that his Autobiography (1947) became a best-seller. He was knighted in 1967, the first music critic to be so honored. Yet only one of his books is now in print, and his vast body of writings on music is unknown save to specialists.
Is there any chance that Cardus's criticism will enjoy a revival? The prospect seems remote. Journalistic tastes had changed long before his death, and postmodern readers have little use for the richly upholstered Vicwardian prose in which he specialized. Moreover, the amateur tradition in music criticism has been in headlong retreat.
1.It is indicated in Paragraphs 1 and 2 that（  ）.
2.Newspaper reviews in England before World War II were characterized by
（  ）.
3.Which of the following would Shaw and Newman most probably agree on?
4.What can be learned about Cardus according to the last two paragraphs?
5.What would be the best title for the text?

options.A: arts criticism has disappeared from big-city newspapers
options.B: English-language newspapers used to carry more arts reviews
options.C: high-quality newspapers retain a large body of readers
options.D: young readers doubt the suitability of criticism on dailies

sub_questions.analysis: 1.应选［B］。考查考生把握“论点一论证”关系的能力。【试题解析】（1）定位在第一、二自然段。第一段陈述“英语报纸艺术评论发生了衰退（decline in the scope and seriousness of their arts coverage） ”，暗示过去曾经十分繁盛。此外，第二段提供了具体信息， 许多都市报“过去可以读到”（could be found） “艺术评论”（arts criticism），而且“当年的面向普通读者的日报适合（suitable）刊登评论”，这些内容具体说明了艺术评论过去多而现在少的现象。（2）这两个段落反复强调的关键概念有“arts coverage, critics”等。综合这些信息，选项［B］最佳。2.应选［A］。考查考生概括文章信息的能力。【试题解析】（1）首先定位在第三段第一句，“报纸评论话题包罗万象（unfocused newspaper reviews） ”，为该段的中心句（topic sentence）。（2）从第三、四自然段也可知，卡杜斯曾经写过关于音乐（music）、板球（cricket）等的评论，说明评论内容广泛。（3）全文的第一句（中心议题句）就提到了“范围（scope）”，由此也可推知本文侧重的是“话题范围的变化”。因此，选项［A］最佳。3.应选［D］。考查考生理解言外之意的能力。【试题解析】（1）根据第三段最后一句，“只有少数的人能坚持到底（to keep their own end up）”，可 以推出“并非所有作家都能胜任新闻写作”。（2）该选项中的“作家”（writers）和“新闻评论”（journalistic writing）都是文章的关键词（文章的重点信息）。与其他选项相比，选项［D］表述最合理。4.应选［A］。考查考生概括原文段间信息的能力。【试题解析】（1）第四段提到：“除了专业人士以外，他写的大量音乐评论文章今天却无人知晓（is unknown save to specialists）”。第五段提到：“新闻的审美情趣已经改变，后现代的读者几乎不需要（have little use for）他所擅长的辞藻华丽的散文（prose）”。（ 2）根据全文的中心议题句，过去风靡的评论（criticism）风格已经衰退（decline），而卡杜斯正是这个论断的例证。综合这些信息，选项［A］最佳。5.应选［B］。考查考生对全文主旨的理解能力。【试题解析】（1）概括各段的信息：第一、二段指出，过去这些年英文报纸上艺术评论的广度和严肃性都在“衰退”（decline）。第四段以卡杜斯为例说明一些艺术评论家被人们“忘却”（virtually forgotten）。第五段指出，由于人们品味的变化，像卡杜斯那样的艺术评论“不太会重现了（The prospect seems remote.）”。（2）全文的中心议题句表明本文中心概念为“newspaper and arts coverage”。（ 3）作者在字里行间流露出对于“衰退（decline）”的哀伤，使用了诸如“inexorable”（无情的，势不可挡的），“retreat”（退场）等词语。综合这些信息，选项［B］最佳。

options.A: It is writers' duty to fulfill journalistic goals.
options.B: It is contemptible for writers to be journalists.
options.C: Writers are likely to be tempted into journalism.
options.D: Not all writers are capable of journalistic writing.

options.A: His music criticism may not appeal to readers today.
options.B: His reputation as a music critic has long been in dispute.
options.C: His style caters largely to modern specialists.
options.D: His writings fail to follow the amateur tradition.

options.A: Newspapers of the Good Old Days

questions.article:
Over the past decade, thousands of patents have been granted for what are called business methods. Amazon.com received one for its “one-click” online payment system. Merrill Lynch got legal protection for an asset allocation strategy. One inventor patented a technique for lifting a box.
Now the nation's top patent court appears completely ready to scale back on business-method patents, which have been controversial ever since they were first authorized 10 years ago. In a move that has intellectual-property lawyers abuzz, the U.S. Court of Appeals for the Federal Circuit said it would use a particular case to conduct a broad review of business-method patents. In re Bilski, as the case is known , is “a very big deal”, says Dennis D. Crouch of the University of Missouri School of Law. It “has the potential to eliminate an entire class of patents.”
Curbs on business-method claims would be a dramatic about-face, because it was the Federal Circuit itself that introduced such patents with its 1998 decision in the so-called State Street Bank case, approving a patent on a way of pooling mutual-fund assets. That ruling produced an explosion in business-method patent filings, initially by emerging Internet companies trying to stake out exclusive rights to specific types of online transactions. Later, more established companies raced to add such patents to their files, if only as a defensive move against rivals that might beat them to the punch. In 2005, IBM noted in a court filing that it had been issued more than 300 business-method patents, despite the fact that it questioned the legal basis for granting them. Similarly, some Wall Street investment firms armed themselves with patents for financial products, even as they took positions in court cases opposing the practice.
The Bilski case involves a claimed patent on a method for hedging risk in the energy market. The Federal Circuit issued an unusual order stating that the case would be heard by all 12 of the court's judges, rather than a typical panel of three, and that one issue it wants to evaluate is whether it should “reconsider” its State Street Bank ruling.
The Federal Circuit's action comes in the wake of a series of recent decisions by the Supreme Court that has narrowed the scope of protections for patent holders. Last April, for example, the justices signaled that too many patents were being upheld for “inventions” that are obvious. The judges on the Federal Circuit are “reacting to the anti-patent trend at the Supreme Court”, says Harold C. Wegner, a patent attorney and professor at George Washington University Law School.
1.Business-method patents have recently aroused concern because of（  ）.
2.Which of the following is true of the Bilski case?
3.The word “about-face” (Line 1, Para 3) most probably means
（  ）.
4.We learn from the last two paragraphs that business-method patents
（  ）.
5.Which of the following would be the subject of the text?

options.C: the possible restriction on their granting

sub_questions.analysis: 1.应选［C］。考查考生理解句际之间因果关系的能力。【试题解析】（1）题干限制的是“最近（recently）”的情形，而非过去的情形。（2）定位在第二段。题干中的“concern”与段中的“controversial”和“abuzz”照应。根据原文“举措引起了知识产权律师们的争议（a move that has...lawyers abuzz）”，之后解释了“举措”（move）的含义，即法庭将“展开对商业模式专利的全面复审”（to conduct a broad review of business-method patents）。（3）第三自然段首句承上启下，“限制商业模式的专利申请（curbs）”再次说明对专利将采取限制措施。综合这些信息，选项［C］最佳。2.应选［D］。考查考生把握原文论点和论据之间关系的能力。【试题解析】（1）可定位在第二自然段末，“正如人们所知道的那样，这一案件事关重大（a very big deal）”，它“有可能导致整类的专利被撤销”（has the potential to eliminate an entire class of patents）。“has the potential”语气和选项中的“may”一致，可见这个案件影响之大。（2）第四段再次提及了该案件的情况，“法庭颁布了一条异乎寻常的法令（an unusual order）”，改变了以前的做法，对专利的审核更加的严格。（3）通过全文的中心议题进行推导：“法庭将大规模缩减商业模式方面的专利（to scale back on business-method patents）”，而比尔斯基案正是说明中心主旨的，意味着司法做法将会改变。综合这些信息，选项［D］最佳。3.应选［C］。考查考生在语境中推测词义的能力。【试题解析】（1） “about-face”的本义为“翻脸”。（2）根据第二段，法庭“将大规模缩减商业模式方面的专利（to scale back on）”，这改变了第一段提到的以前的做法。第三段正好对上文内容进行了评论，说明“态度急剧转变”（would be a dramatic about-face）的缘由。（3）本文篇首通过叙事变化，暗示着形势发生了改变（change）。综合这些信息，选项［C］最佳。4.应选［B］。考查考生理解段落之间关系的能力。【试题解析】（1）第五自然段为第四自然段提供了背景，最近最高法院做出了一系列判决，缩小专利保护范围，随之而来的是巡回法庭要重审甚至可能撤销一些商业模式专利（should "reconsider" its State Street Bank ruling）。法官发出信号：“太多的（too many）商业模式专利发明，太明了，无须专利保护”。换言之， 这些专利没有必要（unnecessary）。（2）其实“granted, issued”本身就是文章的关键词（反复出现的概念）。（3）从作者的立场看，选项［B］能够体现其评价和观点。综合这些信息，选项［B］最佳。5.应选［A］。考查考生综合理解文章主旨、作者态度的能力。【试题解析】（1）概括各个段落的话题：文章第一段陈述了商业模式专利十年来被广泛授予的现象；第二段指出，专利法庭准备限制甚至取消商业模式专利，接下来作者补充说明商业模式专利在美国的发展情况，以及法庭态度的巨大转变；最后两段解释商业模式专利在美国的现状和改变的理由。（2）抓住全文的中心主旨（第二段第一句）。（3）选项［A］中的“looming”常表示“耸然、隐约、模糊地出现”，与文中的态度急转（about-face）、可能性大（would，potential）以及预示性（signaled）等词在意思上更贴近。综合这些信息，选项［A］最佳。

questions.article:
In his book The Tipping Point, Malcolm Gladwell argues that “social epidemics” are driven in large part by the actions of a tiny minority of special individuals, often called influentials, who are unusually informed, persuasive, or well connected. The idea is intuitively compelling, but it doesn't explain how ideas actually spread.
The supposed importance of influentials derives from a plausible-sounding but largely untested theory called the “two-step flow of communication”: Information flows from the media to the influentials and from them to everyone else. Marketers have embraced the two-step flow because it suggests that if they can just find and influence the influentials, those selected people will do most of the work for them. The theory also seems to explain the sudden and unexpected popularity of certain looks, brands, or neighborhoods. In many such cases, a cursory search for causes finds that some small group of people was wearing, promoting, or developing whatever it is before anyone else paid attention. Anecdotal evidence of this kind fits nicely with the idea that only certain special people can drive trends. In their recent work, however, some researchers have come up with the finding that influentials have far less impact on social epidemics than is generally supposed. In fact, they don't seem to be required at all.
The researchers' argument stems from a simple observation about social influence: with the exception of a few celebrities like Oprah Winfrey—whose outsize presence is primarily a function of media, not interpersonal, influence—even the most influential members of a population simply don't interact with that many others. Yet it is precisely these non-celebrity influentials who, according to the two-step-flow theory, are supposed to drive social epidemics, by influencing their friends and colleagues directly. For a social epidemic to occur, however, each person so affected, must then influence his or her own acquaintances, who must in turn influence theirs, and so on; and just how many others pay attention to each of these people has little to do with the initial influential. If people in the network just two degrees removed from the initial influential prove resistant, for example, the cascade of change won't propagate very far or affect many people.
Building on the basic truth about interpersonal influence, the researchers studied the dynamics of social influence by conducting thousands of computer simulations of populations, manipulating a number of variables relating to people's ability to influence others and their tendency to be influenced. They found that the principal requirement for what is called “global cascades” — the widespread propagation of influence through networks—is the presence not of a few influentials but, rather, of a critical mass of easily influenced people.
1.By citing the book The Tipping Point, the author intends to（  ）.
2.The author suggests that the "two-step-flow theory"
（  ）.
3.What the researchers have observed recently shows that
（  ）.
4.The underlined phrase “these people” in paragraph 4 refers to the ones who
（  ）.
5.What is the essential element in the dynamics of social influence?

options.A: analyze the consequences of social epidemics
options.B: discuss influentials' function in spreading ideas
options.C: exemplify people's intuitive response to social epidemics
options.D: describe the essential characteristics of influentials

options.A: serves as a solution to marketing problems
options.B: has helped explain certain prevalent trends
options.D: requires solid evidence for its validity

options.B: interpersonal links can be enhanced through the media
options.C: influentials have more channels to reach the public
options.D: most celebrities enjoy wide media attention

options.B: have little contact with the source of influence
options.C: are influenced and then influence others
options.D: are influenced by the initial influential
